intended_use: Clarius OB AI is intended to assist in measurements of fetal biometric parameters (i.e., head circumference, abdominal circumference, femur length, bi-parietal diameter, crown rump length) on ultrasou
intended_use_full: Clarius OB AI is intended to assist in measurements of fetal biometric parameters (i.e., head circumference, abdominal circumference, femur length, bi-parietal diameter, crown rump length) on ultrasound data acquired by the Clarius Ultrasound Scanner (i.e., curvilinear scanner). The user shall be a healthcare professional trained and qualified in ultrasound. The user retains the responsibility of confirming the validity of the measurements based on standard practices and clinical judgment. Clarius OB AI is indicated for use in adult patients only.
target_condition: Obstetric/fetal imaging; assessment of fetal anatomy and biometrics for calculation of gestational age
ground_truth: Manual measurements performed by 3 expert clinicians with relevant obstetric/fetal ultrasound experience, with each image reviewed by 3 blinded reviewers; segmentation boundaries outlined by clinical reviewers
other_metrics: Intraclass correlation coefficient (ICC) for inter-rater reliability: 0.99 (95% CI 0.994–0.997); Average Jaccard scores range: 0.73 (95% CI 0.72–0.74) to 0.94 (95% CI 0.93–0.94); Average Dice scores range: 0.84 (95% CI 0.83–0.87) to 0.97 (95% CI 0.96–0.97); Statistical significance p-value <2.2 e-16 for non-inferiority testing; Clarius OB AI found to be non-inferior to human experts
